jokes.text: Ab, Ab, Ab-- No joke, today is the day we become Abbi and Ilana, the boss bitches we are in our minds.
jokes.setup: Ilana declares this is their day to become the powerful women they imagine themselves to be
jokes.punchline: The qualifier 'in our minds' reveals they're delusional about their actual status
jokes.explanation: Strong character-establishing moment with good ironic twist. The gap between fantasy and reality is classic comedy.

jokes.text: I'm getting big ol' balls of mozzarella today. Chea-- Get it! Chea-- Harder.
jokes.setup: Ilana needs to explain her plan to get money
jokes.punchline: She uses 'balls of mozzarella' as slang for getting paid, with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Bizarre food-as-money metaphor is distinctly Ilana. The sexual undertones add to character authenticity.

jokes.text: You're gonna have to speak in English for me.
jokes.setup: Ilana just delivered a confusing metaphor about mozzarella
jokes.punchline: Abbi's deadpan response highlights how incomprehensible Ilana can be
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-woman response. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: I bet you schedule when you jack off.
jokes.setup: Ilana criticizes Abbi for being stuck in routine
jokes.punchline: Escalates to an absurdly intimate accusation about scheduling masturbation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Ilana's boundary-crossing personality. Shock value is earned through character truth.

jokes.text: Schedule when I ja-- (exhales deeply)
jokes.setup: Abbi starts to repeat Ilana's accusation
jokes.punchline: She can't even finish the sentence, showing her mortification
jokes.explanation: Excellent reaction beat. The incomplete sentence and exhale perfectly capture Abbi's discomfort.

jokes.text: I'm just keeping him warm.
jokes.setup: Abbi asks if Lincoln is inside Ilana during their video call
jokes.punchline: Ilana gives a casual, domestic explanation for having sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement. Treating sex like warming a cup of coffee is absurd and economical.

jokes.text: Thank you-- I've been doing this no-bread diet and it's pretty good. It's been working.
jokes.setup: Abbi politely says Lincoln seems well
jokes.punchline: He responds earnestly about his diet while literally having sex
jokes.explanation: The casual normalcy of diet talk during sex is perfectly absurd and character-driven.

jokes.text: That was like a threesome, in a way.
jokes.setup: Lincoln leaves after the awkward video call
jokes.punchline: Ilana reframes the uncomfortable moment as sexually adventurous
jokes.explanation: Pure Ilana logic - turning an awkward moment into something she sees as positive and sexual.

jokes.text: Try not to make eye contact with any members when you're holding the plunger. Really bums 'em out.
jokes.setup: Tre assigns Abbi another toilet cleaning job
jokes.punchline: His casual advice about the psychological impact of seeing the cleaning lady
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor about gym class dynamics. The casual delivery sells it.

jokes.text: I think it's the scent of a deal.
jokes.setup: Todd asks if people smell something
jokes.punchline: He turns a bad smell into corporate motivation speak
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate bro culture, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: I've been kind of obsessed with getting paid and I was wondering if that's happening today so we could all be paid.
jokes.setup: Todd asks Ilana for her business deal idea
jokes.punchline: She treats getting her paycheck like a business proposal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion. Using corporate speak to ask for basic employment rights is perfect satire.

jokes.text: Speaking of F-M-L, went on a third date with that girl from Match.com, to keep you guys updated, third time, went for the kiss, third time rejected, but I'll try-- I'll get it.
jokes.setup: Todd transitions from work disappointment
jokes.punchline: He overshares about his dating failures with inappropriate confidence
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward boss character moment. Functional but not innovative.

jokes.text: That's the look of a dealer right there. If I was in Las Vegas, I'd assume you could move the table limit. That's how much of a dealer you are.
jokes.setup: Man sees Ilana on computer
jokes.punchline: He interprets her concentration as gambling expertise with elaborate metaphor
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific metaphor works but feels disconnected from main characters.

jokes.text: Don't Black Swan me like that, dude.
jokes.setup: Abbi startles Ilana
jokes.punchline: Ilana turns the movie title into a verb about being scared
jokes.explanation: Creative verb formation from pop culture. Economical and memorable.

jokes.text: It's called the non-member pen and you know that.
jokes.setup: Ilana calls it the 'second-class citizen area'
jokes.punchline: Abbi corrects with the official gym terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Abbi's buy-in to workplace hierarchy.

jokes.text: I think you deserve like an Abbi Bueller's day off.
jokes.setup: Ilana wants Abbi to leave work
jokes.punchline: She personalizes the movie title with Abbi's name
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective personalization of classic reference. Shows their friendship dynamic.

jokes.text: No, they don't. They let me do a lot more than that. I wash the towels and then I dry the towels.
jokes.setup: Ilana says they only let Abbi fold towels
jokes.punchline: Abbi's defense actually proves Ilana's point about her limited duties
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment where Abbi's defense undermines itself. Great ironic structure.

jokes.text: We got another pube situation in the women's locker room. Yeah-- Somebody really went to town in the second shower stall.
jokes.setup: Tre needs Abbi for another cleaning job
jokes.punchline: The euphemistic description of the disgusting situation
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with euphemistic language. Functional workplace comedy.

jokes.text: You know, you should just label it 'Abbi's snake,' you're so good with it.
jokes.setup: Tre mentions the plumbing snake
jokes.punchline: His suggestion to label it as hers highlights her degrading position
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how Abbi is pigeonholed into the worst jobs.

jokes.text: I actually might be positive. I'm dealing with some pretty scary test results that are at a clinic right now and I have to get 'em, but I'm at work. I mean, I might be positive in a blood situation, so...
jokes.setup: Tre asks Abbi to be positive
jokes.punchline: She interprets 'positive' as HIV-positive and reveals potential health scare
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection turning workplace positivity into genuine fear. Dark but character-driven.

jokes.text: Is it herpes? No, it's not. Genital warts. No.
jokes.setup: Tre tries to guess Abbi's medical issue
jokes.punchline: Each guess gets progressively more uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Standard escalating guessing game. Works but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Let me know if you have AIDS. You'll be the first one that I tell. When I find out.
jokes.setup: Tre shows concern about Abbi's test results
jokes.punchline: Abbi's matter-of-fact promise about sharing such devastating news
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect deadpan. The casual tone makes it funnier and more unsettling.

jokes.text: You took all of his office supplies. Listen, what I do at my place of work is none of your godamn business, okay?
jokes.setup: Abbi points out Ilana's theft
jokes.punchline: Ilana acts indignant about workplace privacy while admitting to theft
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ilana's twisted logic and lack of shame.

jokes.text: Ugh-- This is a slave song. It's not a slave song. Yes, it is. It's widely known by the black community that 'What a Wonderful World' is, like, a slave song.
jokes.setup: They hear 'What a Wonderful World' playing
jokes.punchline: Ilana makes an absurd historical claim about the song
jokes.explanation: Captures Ilana's tendency to make confident but wrong cultural statements. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Right, right, right, I totally forgot that you're, like, the voice of the whole black community.
jokes.setup: Ilana claims to know what the black community thinks
jokes.punchline: Abbi sarcastically calls out Ilana's presumption
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that highlights both characters perfectly. Abbi's rare moment of sarcastic superiority.

jokes.text: I would like to return these items. I once owned them personally, but I have no use for them now, so.
jokes.setup: Ilana approaches a store return counter
jokes.punchline: Her formal language barely disguises that she's returning stolen items
jokes.explanation: The overly formal language to describe obvious fraud is perfectly Ilana.

jokes.text: You know, we'd love to get that in liquid cash.
jokes.setup: She gets store credit for her returns
jokes.punchline: She uses 'liquid cash' like financial jargon for actual money
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that shows Ilana trying to sound sophisticated while begging.

jokes.text: So I noticed that you ate all of my cheese again. Could you not do that? Or maybe get more?
jokes.setup: Ilana confronts Bevers about eating her food
jokes.punchline: Her polite, reasonable tone about an ongoing problem
jokes.explanation: Shows Ilana's attempt at mature conflict resolution in an absurd situation.

jokes.text: Maybe in the future if you do that, put it on, like-- Like, all six sides of the cheese brick.
jokes.setup: Bevers admits he didn't realize the labeled cheese was hers
jokes.punchline: He suggests an impractical labeling solution
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd logic. The specificity of 'all six sides' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're not my roommate. You're my roommate's boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Tension builds in the cheese argument
jokes.punchline: Ilana clarifies the power dynamic in her apartment
jokes.explanation: Good establishment of the household hierarchy and source of conflict.

jokes.text: I do know that, but I also know that you told me not to touch your stuff in the bathroom. Right. That's my mistake.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the clogged toilet and available plunger
jokes.punchline: Bevers uses Ilana's own rules against her, forcing her to take blame
jokes.explanation: Great example of how Bevers weaponizes Ilana's own reasonable boundaries against her.

jokes.text: You disgust me.
jokes.setup: The entire frustrating exchange with Bevers
jokes.punchline: Ilana's simple, direct expression of her feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect summary of her feelings. The simplicity after the complex argument works well.

jokes.text: Yes, I placed the Jewess ad. Both of us are over 18, yeah. Under 18, I said. We're both under 18, we're young teens--
jokes.setup: Ilana is on the phone trying to make money
jokes.punchline: She lies about their ages for what's clearly an inappropriate ad
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about desperation for money. The age-lying escalation is disturbing but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: It kills me that he lives here and doesn't pay a cent. I feel dirtier when I leave the shower because his hair-- his hair is, like, all over my body.
jokes.setup: Ilana complains about Bevers
jokes.punchline: The specific gross detail about his hair in the shower
jokes.explanation: Good specific gross detail that makes the character complaint more vivid and disgusting.

jokes.text: It's like I have to see your dick in your shorts. God!
jokes.setup: Continuing her rant about Bevers
jokes.punchline: Another unwanted intimate detail about living with him
jokes.explanation: Continues the gross-out theme but less inventive than the hair complaint.

jokes.text: Jeremy. (in slow motion)
jokes.setup: Abbi encounters Jeremy at the gym
jokes.punchline: The transcript indicates slow motion, suggesting romantic comedy parody
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy parody of romantic movie tropes. The slow motion effect is clearly comedic.

jokes.text: Making up a batch of my famous peppercorn and cardamom cider beer. I'm not gonna finish it all myself. You need someone else.
jokes.setup: Jeremy tries to connect with Abbi
jokes.punchline: His weirdly specific hipster beverage creation
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor. The elaborate drink description perfectly captures pretentious Brooklyn guy.

jokes.text: Like a hot cookie? Soft but not moist. Or, surprise me!
jokes.setup: Bevers asks for something sweet
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific and gross cookie preferences
jokes.explanation: The 'soft but not moist' distinction is perfectly weird and specific to Bevers' character.

jokes.text: Kill you in your sleep, how's that for a surprise?
jokes.setup: Bevers asks Abbi to surprise him with treats
jokes.punchline: Abbi's violent fantasy response
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark humor payoff. Shows normally nice Abbi pushed to homicidal thoughts. Great economy.

jokes.text: When you're ready to become a citizen, my legs are wide open.
jokes.setup: Ilana thanks Jaime for being a good roommate
jokes.punchline: She offers sexual favors for immigration help
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Ilana - inappropriate, sexual, and weirdly generous. The phrasing is memorable.

jokes.text: I don't think that's how it works, but thank you.
jokes.setup: Ilana offers sexual citizenship help
jokes.punchline: Jaime's polite but confused response
jokes.explanation: Great straight-man response. The politeness despite the absurdity is perfect.

jokes.text: Why is he doing this to me before curtain?
jokes.setup: Lincoln appears before Ilana's street performance
jokes.punchline: She treats busking like serious theater with 'curtain' terminology
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Ilana's tendency to elevate mundane activities with theatrical language.

jokes.text: I'm gonna respect your dick later.
jokes.setup: Exchange about respecting each other's space
jokes.punchline: Ilana turns 'respect' into sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Classic Ilana move - turning a respectful conversation sexual. Very economical delivery.

jokes.text: The first season of 'Seinfeld,' not that many people saw that show, but it got time to grow.
jokes.setup: Their street performance isn't going well
jokes.punchline: Ilana compares their failure to Seinfeld's early ratings
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about struggling entertainment. The comparison is delusionally optimistic in a funny way.

jokes.text: She should be giving us, like, 25%. We can't do this anymore. This is insane. We're like-- Where'd the music go?
jokes.setup: A breakdancer starts performing to their music
jokes.punchline: They demand a cut of earnings they're not getting, then lose their accompaniment
jokes.explanation: Shows their delusional entitlement and rapid failure. The quick collapse adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I just don't understand why she took the fishbowl. I would have given it to her. Homeless people need supplies, I don't know.
jokes.setup: Their money collection was stolen
jokes.punchline: Abbi tries to rationalize the theft charitably
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Abbi's tendency to see the best in bad situations.

jokes.text: It's PCP.
jokes.setup: Abbi compliments the pot quality
jokes.punchline: Ilana reveals it's actually a dangerous drug
jokes.explanation: Good shock revelation. Short and effective escalation that explains their behavior.

jokes.text: We're just two gals, cleaning in our underwear for an hour.
jokes.setup: Abbi realizes what the job actually involves
jokes.punchline: Ilana's casual description of sex work
jokes.explanation: The casual 'gals' and understated description makes the inappropriate request funnier.

jokes.text: I'm gonna (bleep) him, dude. Who's Weezy? Lil Wayne! This is the whole thing!
jokes.setup: Ilana explains her motivation
jokes.punchline: Her sexual goal with the rapper and Abbi's confusion about the nickname
jokes.explanation: Shows the disconnect between Ilana's grand plans and Abbi's awareness. Character-consistent.

jokes.text: What would you be doing instead? Watching 'Breaking Bad' and eating a Smart Ones? Okay, it's a cashew stir-fry and I was, like, really looking forward to it.
jokes.setup: Ilana criticizes Abbi's alternative plans
jokes.punchline: Abbi's defensive correction about her sad dinner plans
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast. Abbi's defensive specificity about her boring plans is endearing.

jokes.text: I'm sowwy. I don't have any. Are you joking right now? I'm a wittle baby. Uh... No, you're a full grown man. I'm a baby, I have no money!
jokes.setup: They demand payment for cleaning
jokes.punchline: The man commits to an infantile persona to avoid paying
jokes.explanation: Bizarre commitment to character to avoid responsibility. The baby voice escalation is absurd.

jokes.text: Does baby like this, huh? Babies do not read books yet! Are you a baby if you wear a hat? Full coat with fur? Babies don't have little... babies!
jokes.setup: They attack the man's baby persona
jokes.punchline: Increasingly frantic attempts to disprove his babyhood
jokes.explanation: Great escalating logic trying to destroy his delusion. The 'little babies' reference is particularly funny.

jokes.text: Are you my fwends? No.
jokes.setup: The man asks if they're his friends in baby voice
jokes.punchline: Ilana's flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response. The simple 'no' after all the chaos is very effective.

jokes.text: Lil Wayne's so short, though, that we weren't even gonna be able to see him from where we would have sat.
jokes.setup: They're consoling themselves about missing the concert
jokes.punchline: Abbi's practical concern about sightlines
jokes.explanation: Good rationalization humor. Very practical Abbi concern about concert logistics.

jokes.text: Do you know me, how I have to pee, like, constantly? We would be standing in line the whole concert. The whole time, we would be standing in a line.
jokes.setup: Continuing to rationalize missing the concert
jokes.punchline: Abbi's specific bathroom anxiety about concerts
jokes.explanation: Very specific Abbi anxiety. The repetition about lines emphasizes her practical worries.

jokes.text: I know that tomorrow... tomorrow's totally the day. Like, I'm so confident in myself, I feel it in my heart. I'm confident myself in the day.
jokes.setup: Abbi tries to be optimistic about the future
jokes.punchline: Her grammar breaks down as she gets more confident
jokes.explanation: The deteriorating grammar as she gets more enthusiastic is a nice touch showing her scattered state.

jokes.text: Dude, I would follow you into hell, brother. I would take you on my shoulders, like, I'd strap you up and I'd be like, let's go through hell.
jokes.setup: Expressing loyalty to Abbi
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate and physical descriptions of devotion
jokes.explanation: Good escalating friendship devotion. The physical imagery of carrying her is sweetly absurd.

jokes.text: Please. Help me. Ay, mi amor, what happened to you?
jokes.setup: Abbi arrives home disheveled from their adventures
jokes.punchline: Jaime's concerned reaction implies she looks terrible
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy moment implied through dialogue. Shows the toll of their day.

jokes.text: Oh, it's happening. Why are we doing this right now? Hymes, you can go. She got me. I don't got her-- I'm not there with you.
jokes.setup: They're getting high and Ilana starts panicking
jokes.punchline: Ilana's scattered, paranoid drug talk
jokes.explanation: Shows the consequences of taking PCP. The scattered dialogue conveys drug effects well.

jokes.text: It's fine, everyone has nights like this. Just, if you feel like you're gonna throw up, just give me a heads up.
jokes.setup: Ilana is having a bad drug experience
jokes.punchline: Abbi's matter-of-fact acceptance and practical concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect supportive friend moment. The understated 'everyone has nights like this' is very Abbi.
